title: asperula glomerata subsp. bracteata
intro: Asperula glomerata subsp. bracteata, commonly known as Wood Sorrel, is a species of perennial plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Asperula glomerata subsp. bracteata include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as supporting digestive health and acting as an antacid. Asperula glomerata subsp. bracteata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
